entry.title: Provisioning Access After Ending Implementation Partner Engagement
entry.content:
We have recently ended our partnership with our SuccessFactors implementation partner.

Going forward, if any changes are required through Provisioning, could you please advise on the available options? We have a certified SuccessFactors consultant within our organization.

We would like to understand:

  1. Whether SAP can support us directly with Provisioning-related changes upon request, or

  2. Whether our in-house certified consultant can be granted access to Provisioning for our company’s SuccessFactors instances, subject to SAP approval.

Please let us know the recommended process and any prerequisites required.

Thank you for your support.

entry.title: Clarification on Long‑Term Safety of Using Custom Wrapper Classes for Compact Styling in Fiori Apps
entry.content:
Hello, For one of our Fiori applications, we need a more compact layout (smaller font size, reduced padding/margins, tighter table rows, etc...). To achieve this while staying within SAP‑recommended guidelines, we avoided modifying standard SAP classes.  Instead, we applied custom wrapper classes such as:  

CSS

 

controller.js

These custom classes are then added only to our List Report or Object Page root elements.

Before we continue with this approach for the long term, I would like to confirm:

  1. Is this approach (using custom wrapper/root classes around standard SAP controls) fully upgrade‑safe according to SAP guidelines?
    We are not changing .sapMBtn, .sapMLabel, .sapMTitle, or other standard selectors directly — only inside our own scope classes.

  2. Does SAP recommend any alternative technique for global compact styling of standard Fiori Elements applications?

entry.title: How do I find out the uncompressed database size?
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

can someone tell me how to find the uncompressed database size for an ERP system (HANA DB)? DBACOCKPIT (Configuration/Volume) only displays the compressed size. The compression factor would also be helpful.

Thanks in advance.

entry.title: High CVE-2001-1464, CVE-2025-5222, and CVE-2020-10531 in Crystal Reports for Eclipse SP32
entry.content:
Crystal Reports for Eclipse SP32 released on Jan 16, 2026, has the following high severity vulnerabilities:

CR4ERL32_0-80004572.ZIP: CrystalCommon2.jar - CVE-2001-1464
CR4ERL32_0-80004572.ZIP: CrystalReportsRuntime.jar - CVE-2001-1464
CR4ERL32_0-80004572.ZIP: webreporting.jar - CVE-2001-1464
CR4ERL32_0-80004572.ZIP: icu4j.jar - CVE-2020-10531
CR4ERL32_0-80004572.ZIP: icu4j.jar - CVE-2025-5222

Please indicate when these will be fixed or provide confirmation that they are false positives.

CRJAVA HIGH CVE

entry.title: For a PL account need to change alternate account
entry.content: For Gl 3408099- Services - Manually there is balance in FBl3n corresponding to Fiscal Years 2011,2014, but Fs10N balance for the GL- Showing as NO data exists for Fy 2026, 2025, 2024 this is a PL account-- I am unable to change Alternate account. Please suggest

entry.title: Looking for Expert and Community Insights on My SAP Technical Journey
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I hope you are all doing well.

I would like to kindly ask for your feedback and professional insights regarding my current learning journey within the SAP Technical domain. My goal is to continuously improve my technical profile and ensure that my learning path aligns well with real-world SAP projects and current market expectations.

Below is a brief overview of my learning direction and the reasoning behind each focus area.


SAP Technical Learning Direction At this stage, my primary focus is on pursuing the SAP Technical track. I am currently concentrating on SAP Basis, aiming to build a strong and solid foundation in SAP system administration, core architecture, and operational concepts.
In parallel, I am expanding my knowledge across several complementary technical areas, as outlined below:


SAP HANA
My objective is to gain a clear and comprehensive understanding of SAP HANA database architecture and how SAP systems interact with the database layer in terms of structure, performance optimization, and system integration.


SAP ABAP
I am working on building a solid understanding of the ABAP stack, with a focus on how development processes are designed and implemented within SAP systems. This helps me better understand system logic, debugging, and collaboration with development teams from a technical perspective.


SAP CPI (Cloud Integration)
My focus includes understanding SAP’s transition from traditional on-premise environments to cloud-based platforms, along with cloud integration concepts such as internal (A2A) and external (B2B) integration scenarios.

Additionally, I have complemented this learning path with the Apache Camel Framework, due to its importance and relevance within SAP integration solutions.


SAP UI5 / Fiori & SAP BTP CAPM
Recently, SAP introduced important updates related to both frontend and backend development models. I have been exploring these updates and following commonly recommended learning resources to better understand the modern SAP development approach, including CAP-based application development.


Learning Approach
Overall, I am intentionally following a structured and methodical learning sequence to build a holistic understanding of SAP Architecture as an integrated enterprise platform, rather than focusing on individual tools or isolated technologies.


Current Learning Path & Courses
Below are the main courses I am currently following (via Udemy):

SAP NetWeaver Fundamentals

SAP Basis Administration

SAP HANA Administration

Apache Camel Framework with Spring Boot

SAP Cloud Integration (CI / CPI), including API Management and Event Mesh

SAP UI5 / Fiori Full Stack with TypeScript / JavaScript using SAP BTP RAPM / CAPM


I would truly appreciate your feedback on whether this learning path is well-structured and aligned with real-world SAP implementations, and whether there are any key areas you would recommend adding, adjusting, or prioritizing differently.

Thank you in advance for your time and valuable insights.

Best regards,

entry.title: Update of Reporting Dashboards in SAP for me results in errors
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We are trying to view the latest Reporting Dashboards in SAP for me, however the data displayed for us is from November and the update is constantly resulting in errors. 

I hope this is the correct place to submit issues with SAP for me.

Thanks

Thomas

entry.title: Adaptation editor not showing navigation in Audit Universe
entry.content: I have created an adaptation project in BAS. However, upon opening the adaptation editor, I cannot see any of the data to make me navigate to the view in where I have to extend. Is this a limitation or am I missing actual roles? I already have all dev roles necessary and auditor roles.

entry.title: SAP Macros Table - Get Selected Table Row Data
entry.content:
Hello. I am trying to retrieve the data from a table row of my SAP Macros table(sap.fe.macros.Table). Application is running a odata V4 using the Flexible Programming Model. I was able to read some SAP documentation on the getObject() function. When I use this, it seems to only retrieve the data from visible columns, but not hidden ones. Is that by design? Or is there another way I can retrieve everything from the row? 

For Example:

Columns A-D are visible in my application, and oSelectedData = {1, 2, 3, 4}. So only the visible columns. Columns E-H are hidden, and the values are not returned. I was expecting to have {1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8}. 

ABCDEFGH
12345678
//Grab Table object
const oTable = this.byId("Table");

//Get selected contexts
const oSelectedContexts = oTable.getSelectedContexts();
const oContext = oSelectedContexts[0];

//Get table row data 
const oSelectedData = oSelectedContexts[0].getObject();

entry.title: Static URL of the Joule application
entry.content:
As part of activating the Joule plugin in our S/4HANA PCE (on-premise) Fiori LaunchPad I have created a Fiori catalog including a SAPUI5 Fiori App (plugin for Joule) as described in the following SAP Help portal documentation; Configure and Activate Joule Plug-In in a Target Mapping | SAP Help Portal.

The above solution design does not seem to match the fact that we have a Joule subscription/application in each of our four BTP subaccounts that are connected to our four respective S/4 HANA PCE (on-premise) systems (sandbox, development, test, production).

Because, this again means that we have four different URL's for this Joule plugin app:

https://<sandbox subdomain>.eu10.sapdas.cloud.sap/resources/public/webclient/s4
https://<development subdomain>.eu10.sapdas.cloud.sap/resources/public/webclient/s4
https://<test subdomain>.eu10.sapdas.cloud.sap/resources/public/webclient/s4
https://<production subdomain>.eu10.sapdas.cloud.sap/resources/public/webclient/s4

So, finally, this will result in having system specific Fiori catalogs and related roles?!

Is there any way around this?

entry.title: CC07 is not displaying Details in right content field
entry.content:
When upgrade to S4 HANA, we can't display change details in CC07 right content area. 

Can you help me check any reason or if there is any other transactions can use in S4?

entry.title: UI5 MessageStrip Error: Assertion failed: Method 'class' must be called with exactly one class name
entry.content:
Hi,

I had an exception in the Console when loading a ui5 page (not Fiori elements), that contains a MessageStrip:

Error: Assertion failed: Method 'class' must be called with exactly one class name

Component: sap.m.MessageStrip
Version affected: 1.143.0+
Version working: 1.142.6
Error: Assertion failed: Method 'class' must be called with exactly one class name
Reproduction: <MessageStrip class="sapUiSmallMargin" text="Test"/>

There are clearly not 2 classes assigned to the MessageStrip. It even occurs if the class tag is not even there.

entry.title: UI5 MessageStrip - Assertion failed: Method 'class' must be called with exactly one class name
entry.content:
Hi, 

I discovered this in a UI5 webapp (not using Fiori Elements) while developing. 

Component: sap.m.MessageStrip
Version affected: 1.143.0+ (until current, 1.144.1)
Version working: 1.142.6
Error: Assertion failed: Method 'class' must be called with exactly one class name
Reproduction: <MessageStrip class="sapUiSmallMargin" text="Test"/>

Even removing the class attribute triggers this failure. There is no MessageStrip in the code, that has more than one CSS class element. There is furthermore no usage of addStyleClass() etc. in the controller logic.

entry.title: Generic actions in custom pages and building blocks (FPM)
entry.content:
Hi, 

I am using Flexible Programming Model for my Fiori Application with RAP in Backend. The initial plan was to use the custom pages and building blocks ( as opposed to Fiori Elements Floorplans List Report and Object Page) .  I added a macros:Form  within a Custom Page (using sap.m.Page, but sap.fe.macros.Page does not work either) to display fields for each business object. But the generic actions like Edit,Delete, Save, Discard Draft are not visible on the UI. Once I create an Object page and the form that way, those generic actions are available and working. My question is if it absolutely necessary to use the floorplans for the CRUD Operations be handled by the framework, or is there a way to make it work without implementing every single generic action manually?

<Page
        id="Invoice"
        title="{i18n>InvoiceTitle}"
    >
       
        <macros:Form id='headData' metaPath="@com.sap.vocabularies.UI.v1.FieldGroup#General"></macros:Form>
 
     
    </Page>

entry.title: User Exit for deriving Characteristics Account Based COPA in SAP S/4Hana
entry.content: We are using Margin Analysis . We are using Rise with SAP. We have created a custom characteristic "WW004". We want to populate this characteristic with Reference Procedure -AWTYP so that it can be used in COPA Report. How to do this?. Any user exit needed?. I want the code for it.

entry.title: Posting not possible in company code 4000 owing to legacy data transfer Message no. AA377
Best Practice for Handling Multiple SAP Backends in UI5 Apps Without Hardcoding URLs
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

I’m currently working on SAP Fiori / UI5 applications that communicate with multiple SAP backend systems via OData, and I’m looking for best practices to avoid hardcoding URLs or system-specific destinations in the app configuration.

Our system landscape contains several backend systems, for example:

  • Production: F51, F52, F53
  • Development / Sandbox: D5W, S5W

Previously, in an on-premise setup, we handled this using system aliases. In UI5 apps or destinations, we referenced something like:

 

destination: D5W_Principal_Propagation

The alias/destination pointed to the appropriate backend system, so we did not need to hardcode real URLs.

 

Current Challenge

With BTP destinations, we want to completely avoid hardcoding URLs in ui5.yaml or in the application code. However, in our scenario:

  • There are multiple backend systems per environment
  • Each system may expose its own OData services
  • Our apps previously relied on alias/destination names to choose the target system

Example of current configuration:

 

backend:
  - path: /sap
    url: http://................................
    destination: D5W_Principal_Propagation

This is DEV-specific and would require manual changes for other systems — which we want to eliminate.

 

Options I’m Considering

 Create one destination per backend system

Example: EWM_F51, EWM_F52, EWM_D5W, etc.

→ The app selects the destination dynamically based on context

 Use one destination per environment and let ABAP/Gateway system aliases handle routing

→ UI5 app calls one logical destination (for example EWM_MAIN)

→ Backend decides which system (F51/F52/F53) processes the request

 Use external configuration to avoid hardcoding destination names in the UI5 app

 

Questions

  • What is the recommended best practice when you have multiple backend systems in the same environment?
  • Is it better to create multiple destinations or rely on backend alias routing?
  • What are best practices for dynamic destination selection in UI5 apps?
  • How do you avoid hardcoding URLs and destination names in large multi-system landscapes?

Any guidance, architecture recommendations, or real project experience would be very helpful.

Thank you.

entry.title: Code Page Conversion Error When Downloading UI5 BSP Apps in S/4HANA (CP1252 Issue)
entry.content:
Hello everyone, and thank you in advance for your support.

We previously worked with the 9.4 system version and used /UI5/UI5_REPOSITORY_LOAD to download UI5 BSP applications without any issues. Now that we are working on an S/4HANA system, we are facing a new problem during the download process.

When we try to download a BSP UI5 app, the report throws the following error:

Code
 
An code page conversion error occurred (path: controller/Worklist-dbg.controller.js / code page: Cp1252)
Warning: Text file ... has not been downloaded

In S/4HANA, the UI5 repository contains the minified .js files along with their .js.map source maps. During the download, the system appears to generate the corresponding *-dbg.js debug versions, and this step triggers a CP1252 code page conversion error, even though the system and SAP GUI are both Unicode. There is also no ZIP download option available in this S/4 environment.

Has anyone encountered this behavior in S/4HANA UI5 apps? Is there an SAP‑recommended workaround for downloading or modifying UI5 controllers when the system generates debug files that cannot be converted?

Thank you for any insights.

entry.title: Translation of User Defined Fields in B1
entry.content:
I have a column in the payment terms (OCTG) called "U_ShowPayment".

I set up values that I can find with 

select * from "UFD1" where "TableID" = 'OCTG';

They have translations that I can merry with

select * from
  "OMLT" T1
  LEFT JOIN "MLT1" T2 ON T2."TranEntry" = T1."TranEntry"
  WHERE T1."TableName" = 'UFD1' AND T1."FieldAlias" = 'Descr'
;

But how can I get from my entry in OCTG to the translation in MLT1? There must be some tables in between. Is there some graphical Documentation showing how those translation tables relate?

entry.title: HTTP 500 Internal Server Error on OData $metadata in SAP Build Work Zone (BTP ABAP Trial)
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am deploying a SAPUI5 application to SAP Build Work Zone using the Managed AppRouter, connected to the BTP ABAP Trial environment.

The app works perfectly locally in BAS, but in Work Zone, it fails to load data. The browser console shows an HTTP 500 Internal Server Error when fetching the OData $metadata and Fiori annotations.

Log-dbg.js:497 2026-02-23 14:21:14.553100 [ODataMetadata] initial loading of metadata failed
Log-dbg.js:497 2026-02-23 14:21:14.553500 Error: HTTP request failed

My Configuration:

  • Destination has HTML5.DynamicDestination = true and WebIDEEnabled = true.

  • Destination has HTML5.ForwardAuthToken = true.

  • My xs-app.json route uses "authenticationType": "xsuaa".

  • I have already cleared the Work Zone site cache.

I have attached my manifest.json, routing file, and a screenshot of my Destination configuration for reference. Any help is greatly appreciated!

manifest.json

{
  "_version": "1.80.0",
  "sap.app": {
    "id": "zcpequipmentapp",
    "type": "application",
    "i18n": "i18n/i18n.properties",
    "applicationVersion": {
      "version": "0.0.1"
    },
    "title": "{{appTitle}}",
    "description": "{{appDescription}}",
    "resources": "resources.json",
    "sourceTemplate": {
      "id": "@sap/generator-fiori:lrop",
      "version": "1.20.2",
      "toolsId": "4c2d36ce-a572-4ef5-a4cb-4bd72a531d54"
    },
    "crossNavigation": {
      "inbounds": {
        "Equipment-manage": {
          "semanticObject": "Equipment",
          "action": "manage",
          "title": "{{appTitle}}",
          "subTitle": "{{appDescription}}",
          "icon": "sap-icon://machine",
          "signature": {
            "parameters": {},
            "additionalParameters": "allowed"
          }
        }
      }
    },
    "dataSources": {
      "ZCP_SB_EQUIP_VAN": {
        "uri": "/sap/opu/odata/IWFND/CATALOGSERVICE;v=2/Annotations(TechnicalName='ZCP_SB_EQUIP_VAN',Version='0001')/$value/",
        "type": "ODataAnnotation",
        "settings": {
          "localUri": "localService/mainService/ZCP_SB_EQUIP_VAN.xml"
        }
      },
      "annotation": {
        "type": "ODataAnnotation",
        "uri": "annotations/annotation.xml",
        "settings": {
          "localUri": "annotations/annotation.xml"
        }
      },
      "mainService": {
        "uri": "/sap/opu/odata/sap/ZCP_SB_EQUIP/",
        "type": "OData",
        "settings": {
          "annotations": ["ZCP_SB_EQUIP_VAN", "annotation"],
          "localUri": "localService/mainService/metadata.xml",
          "odataVersion": "2.0"
        }
      }
    }
  },
  "sap.ui": {
    "technology": "UI5",
    "icons": {
      "icon": "sap-icon://machine",
      "favIcon": "",
      "phone": "",
      "phone@2": "",
      "tablet": "",
      "tablet@2": ""
    },
    "deviceTypes": {
      "desktop": true,
      "tablet": true,
      "phone": true
    }
  },
  "sap.ui5": {
    "flexEnabled": true,
    "dependencies": {
      "minUI5Version": "1.142.6",
      "libs": {
        "sap.m": {},
        "sap.ui.core": {},
        "sap.ushell": {},
        "sap.f": {},
        "sap.ui.comp": {},
        "sap.ui.generic.app": {},
        "sap.suite.ui.generic.template": {}
      }
    },
    "contentDensities": {
      "compact": true,
      "cozy": true
    },
    "models": {
      "i18n": {
        "type": "sap.ui.model.resource.ResourceModel",
        "settings": {
          "bundleName": "zcpequipmentapp.i18n.i18n"
        }
      },
      "": {
        "dataSource": "mainService",
        "preload": true,
        "settings": {
          "defaultBindingMode": "TwoWay",
          "defaultCountMode": "Inline",
          "refreshAfterChange": false,
          "metadataUrlParams": {
            "sap-value-list": "none"
          }
        }
      },
      "@i18n": {
        "type": "sap.ui.model.resource.ResourceModel",
        "uri": "i18n/i18n.properties"
      }
    },
    "services": {
      "ShellUIService": {
        "factoryName": "sap.ushell.ui5service.ShellUIService"
      }
    },
    "resources": {
      "css": []
    },
    "routing": {
      "config": {},
      "routes": [],
      "targets": {}
    }
  },
  "sap.ui.generic.app": {
    "_version": "1.3.0",
    "settings": {
      "forceGlobalRefresh": false,
      "objectPageHeaderType": "Dynamic",
      "considerAnalyticalParameters": true,
      "showDraftToggle": false
    },
    "pages": {
      "ListReport|Equipment": {
        "entitySet": "Equipment",
        "component": {
          "name": "sap.suite.ui.generic.template.ListReport",
          "list": true,
          "settings": {
            "condensedTableLayout": true,
            "smartVariantManagement": true,
            "enableTableFilterInPageVariant": true,
            "filterSettings": {
              "dateSettings": {
                "useDateRange": true
              }
            },
            "tableSettings": {
              "type": "ResponsiveTable"
            }
          }
        },
        "pages": {
          "ObjectPage|Equipment": {
            "entitySet": "Equipment",
            "defaultLayoutTypeIfExternalNavigation": "MidColumnFullScreen",
            "component": {
              "name": "sap.suite.ui.generic.template.ObjectPage"
            }
          }
        }
      }
    }
  },
  "sap.fiori": {
    "registrationIds": ["F1234"],
    "archeType": "transactional"
  },
  "sap.cloud": {
    "public": true,
    "service": "zcpequipmentapp"
  }
}


mta.yaml

_schema-version: "3.2"
ID: zcpequipmentapp
description: Generated by Fiori Tools
version: 0.0.1
modules:
  - name: zcpequipmentapp-destination-content
    type: com.sap.application.content
    requires:
      - name: zcpequipmentapp-destination-service
        parameters:
          content-target: true
      - name: zcpequipmentapp-repo-host
        parameters:
          service-key:
            name: zcpequipmentapp-repo-host-key
      - name: zcpequipmentapp-uaa
        parameters:
          service-key:
            name: zcpequipmentapp-uaa-key
    parameters:
      content:
        instance:
          destinations:
            - Name: zcpequipmentapp_html_repo_host
              ServiceInstanceName: zcpequipmentapp-html5-service
              ServiceKeyName: zcpequipmentapp-repo-host-key
              sap.cloud.service: zcpequipmentapp
            - Authentication: OAuth2UserTokenExchange
              Name: zcpequipmentapp_uaa
              ServiceInstanceName: zcpequipmentapp-xsuaa-service
              ServiceKeyName: zcpequipmentapp-uaa-key
              sap.cloud.service: zcpequipmentapp
          existing_destinations_policy: update
    build-parameters:
      no-source: true

  - name: zcpequipmentapp-app-content
    type: com.sap.application.content
    path: .
    requires:
      - name: zcpequipmentapp-repo-host
        parameters:
          content-target: true
    build-parameters:
      build-result: resources
      requires:
        - artifacts:
            - zcpequipmentapp.zip
          name: zcpequipmentapp
          target-path: resources/

  - name: zcpequipmentapp
    type: html5
    path: .
    build-parameters:
      build-result: dist
      builder: custom
      commands:
        - npm install
        - npm run build:cf
      supported-platforms: []

resources:
  - name: zcpequipmentapp-destination-service
    type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
    parameters:
      config:
        HTML5Runtime_enabled: true
        init_data:
          instance:
            destinations:
              - Authentication: NoAuthentication
                Name: ui5
                ProxyType: Internet
                Type: HTTP
                URL: https://ui5.sap.com
            existing_destinations_policy: update
        version: 1.0.0
      service: destination
      service-name: zcpequipmentapp-destination-service
      service-plan: lite
  - name: zcpequipmentapp-uaa
    type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
    parameters:
      path: ./xs-security.json
      service: xsuaa
      service-name: zcpequipmentapp-xsuaa-service
      service-plan: application
  - name: zcpequipmentapp-repo-host
    type: org.cloudfoundry.managed-service
    parameters:
      service: html5-apps-repo
      service-name: zcpequipmentapp-html5-service
      service-plan: app-host

parameters:
  deploy_mode: html5-repo
  enable-parallel-deployments: true


destination
